stem: Which address prefix range is reserved for IPv4 loopback?
explain_short: 127.0.0.0/8 is reserved for loopback (127.0.0.1 is the typical address used for self-tests).

explain_lecture.slide: Types of IPv4 Addresses — Special Use IPv4 Addresses
explain_lecture.quote: Loopback addresses 127.0.0.0 /8 (127.0.0.1 to 127.255.255.254) Commonly identified as only 127.0.0.1 Used on a host to test if TCP/IP is operational.

stem: A message is sent to dedicated host on a remote network. Which type of message is it?
explain_short: A message sent to one dedicated host (a single specific destination) is a unicast.

explain_lecture.slide: IPv4 Unicast, Broadcast, and Multicast — Unicast
explain_lecture.quote: Unicast transmission is sending a packet to one destination IP address.

stem: From the list below pls specify experimental address?
explain_short: Class E (240.0.0.0–255.255.255.255) is reserved for experimental use. The other choices are public, link-local (APIPA), loopback, and private.

explain_lecture.slide: Types of IPv4 Addresses — Legacy Classful Addressing

stem: A host is transmitting a multicast. Which host or hosts will receive it?
choices: a specially defined group of hosts
the closest neighbor on the same network
explain_short: A multicast packet is delivered only to the specifically defined group of hosts that have joined that multicast group — not all hosts.

explain_lecture.slide: IPv4 Unicast, Broadcast, and Multicast — Multicast
explain_lecture.quote: Multicast transmission is sending a packet to a multicast address group.

stem: What is the purpose of the 'ip helper-address' command on a router sub-interface?
choices: To forward DHCP requests to another VLAN
explain_short: ip helper-address turns a router into a DHCP relay agent — it forwards client broadcast DHCP requests as unicast to a DHCP server on a different subnet/VLAN.

stem: Which three fields aren't used in a UDP segment header?
explain_short: The UDP header has only 4 fields: source port, destination port, length, checksum. So Window Size, Acknowledgment Number, and Sequence Number are NOT in a UDP header (they are TCP-only).

explain_lecture.quote: The UDP header is far simpler than the TCP header because it only has four fields and requires 8 bytes (i.e. 64 bits).

stem: Which two characteristics aren't associated with UDP sessions?
choices: Destination devices receive traffic with minimal delay.
Destination devices reassemble messages and pass them to an application.
explain_short: UDP is connectionless and best-effort: no tracking of segments, and there is no formal session-level reassembly the way TCP performs. Minimal delay and no acknowledgements ARE characteristics of UDP.

explain_lecture.slide: Transportation of Data — User Datagram Protocol (UDP)
explain_lecture.quote: UDP provides the basic functions for delivering datagrams between the appropriate applications, with very little overhead and data checking. UDP is a connectionless protocol.

stem: Which three statements doesn't characterise UDP?
choices: UDP provides connection-oriented, fast transport of data at Layer 3.
UDP relies on application layer protocols for error detection.
UDP is a low overhead protocol that does not provide sequencing or flow control mechanisms.
UDP relies on IP for error detection and recovery.
UDP provides sophisticated flow control mechanisms.
explain_short: UDP is connectionless (NOT connection-oriented), does NOT rely on IP for error detection/recovery, and does NOT provide sophisticated flow control. The other two statements are correct UDP characteristics.

explain_lecture.quote: UDP features include the following: Data is reconstructed in the order that it is received. Any segments that are lost are not resent. There is no session establishment.

stem: Which subnet mask would be used if 5 host bits are available?
explain_short: 5 host bits → mask has 32 − 5 = 27 network bits → /27 = 255.255.255.224.

stem: Which applications or services allow hosts to act as client and server at the same time?
explain_short: Peer-to-peer (P2P) applications (e.g., BitTorrent, eDonkey) let each host act as client and server simultaneously.

explain_lecture.quote: A P2P application allows a device to act as both a client and a server within the same communication.

stem: What is the purpose of the network security authorization function?
choices: to require users to prove who they are
to determine which resources a user can access
to keep track of the actions of a user
to provide challenge and response questions
explain_short: AAA: Authentication = prove who you are; Authorization = what you can do/access; Accounting = track what you did. Authorization controls resource access.

stem: The .......... command is very important to use when log messages appear. Log messages appear by default when a user is directly consoled into a Cisco device, but require this command to be entered when a user is accessing a network device remotely.
explain_short: terminal monitor enables the display of debug/log messages on the current vty (Telnet/SSH) session — by default they only appear on the console.

stem: How many host addresses are available on the 192.168.128.128/27?
explain_short: /27 = 5 host bits → 2^5 − 2 = 30 usable hosts.

stem: A site administrator has been told that a particular network at the site must accommodate 30 hosts. Which subnet mask would be used that contains the required number of host bits?
What route would have the lowest administrative distance?
choices: a route received through the EIGRP routing protocol
a route received through the OSPF routing protocol
explain_short: Directly connected networks have AD = 0, the lowest possible value (most trusted). Static = 1, EIGRP internal = 90, OSPF = 110.

explain_lecture.quote: Directly connected 0, Static route 1, EIGRP summary route 5, External BGP 20, Internal EIGRP 90, OSPF 110, IS-IS 115, RIP 120, External EIGRP 170, Internal BGP 200.

stem: Which OSPF packet contains the different types of link-state advertisements?
explain_short: LSU (Link-State Update) packets carry one or more LSAs. The five OSPF packets are Hello, DBD, LSR, LSU, and LSAck.

stem: What is a disadvantage of NAT?
choices: The router does not need to alter the checksum of the IPv4 packets.
The internal hosts have to use a single public IPv4 address for external communication.
The costs of readdressing hosts can be significant for a publicly addressed network.
explain_short: NAT rewrites IP headers, so true end-to-end addressing (and traceability) is lost. The router must recompute the checksum because of the change.

stem: A client creates a packet to send to a server. The client is requesting SSH service. What number will be used as the destination port number in the sending packet?
explain_short: SSH listens on TCP port 22 for secure remote management of network devices.

stem: What is the difference between a virus and a worm?
choices: Viruses self-replicate but worms do not.
Worms self-replicate but viruses do not.
Worms require a host file but viruses do not.
Viruses hide in legitimate programs but worms do not.
explain_short: Worms self-replicate and spread across networks on their own; viruses require a host file/program and user action to spread.

stem: Which addresses are valid private addresses?

explain_lecture.slide: Types of IPv4 Addresses — Public and Private IPv4 Addresses
explain_lecture.quote: As defined in RFC 1918, public IPv4 addresses are globally routed between internet service provider (ISP) routers. However, private addresses are not globally routable.

stem: Which three protocols operate at the application layer of the TCP/IP model? (Choose three.)
explain_short: FTP, DHCP, and POP3 are all application-layer protocols of the TCP/IP model. ARP operates between L2/L3, TCP and UDP are transport-layer.

explain_lecture.slide: Application, Presentation, and Session — TCP/IP Application Layer Protocols
explain_lecture.quote: Some of the most widely known application layer protocols include HTTP, FTP, TFTP, IMAP and DNS.

stem: A dedicated server is needed when implementing a peer-to-peer network. (true/false)
explain_short: False. In a P2P network there is no dedicated server — every peer can act as both client and server.

explain_lecture.quote: In a peer-to-peer (P2P) network, two or more computers are connected via a network and can share resources (such as printers and files) without having a dedicated server.

stem: What does OSPF use to determine the best path through a network? OSPF uses the cumulative bandwidth of links along the path.
choices: the amount of traffic and the probability of failure of links.
the amount of packet delivery time and lowest bandwidth.
the number of hops along the routing path.
the cumulative bandwidth that is used along the routing path.
explain_short: OSPF computes cost from cumulative bandwidth (cost = 10^8 / BW). Lower total cost = better path. It is a link-state protocol — not hop-count or delivery-time.

stem:
A network administrator is designing an IPv4 addressing scheme and requires these subnets:
1 subnet of 100 hosts
2 subnets of 80 hosts
2 subnets of 30 hosts
4 subnets of 20 hosts

Which combination of subnets and masks will provide the best addressing plan for these requirements?
A) 9 subnets of 126 hosts with 255.255.255.128 mask
choices: 1 subnet of 126 hosts with 255.255.255.128 mask, 2 subnets of 80 hosts with 255.255.255.128 mask, 2 subnets of 30 hosts with 255.255.255.224 mask, 4 subnets of 20 hosts with 255.255.255.224 mask
1 subnet of 126 hosts with 255.255.255.128 mask, 2 subnets of 80 hosts with 255.255.255.128 mask, 2 subnets of 30 hosts with 255.255.255.240 mask, 4 subnets of 20 hosts with 255.255.255.240 mask
1 subnet of 126 hosts with 255.255.255.128 mask, 2 subnets of 80 hosts with 255.255.255.128 mask, 2 subnets of 30 hosts with 255.255.255.224 mask, 4 subnets of 20 hosts with 255.255.255.240 mask
explain_short: Likely D: 100 hosts → /25 (126 hosts), 80 hosts → /25 (126 hosts each), 30 hosts → /27 (30 hosts each), 20 hosts → /27 (30 hosts each). VLSM logic. Need to verify against actual printed paper choices.

stem: Each subnet is the same as a single network. (Subnet planning question — exact stem unclear from photo.)
explain_short: Photo unreadable — could not transcribe choices.

stem: Why does OSPF require a routing process responsible for processing learned routes? (Photo blurred — exact stem and choices unclear.)
explain_short: Photo unreadable for this question — needs manual transcription.

stem: What does OSPF use to create the OSPF neighbor table? (Photo blurred — exact wording unclear.)
explain_short: OSPF maintains an adjacency database (neighbor table) which lists all confirmed neighboring routers with established adjacencies.

stem: What are two functions of a router? (Choose two.)
choices: It controls the flow of data via the use of Layer 2 addresses.
It determines the best path to send packets.
It builds a routing table based on ARP requests.
explain_short: A router determines the best path for packets and connects multiple IP networks (different broadcast/IP domains).

stem: What is an accurate description of redundancy?
choices: configuring a router with a complete MAC address database to ensure that all frames can be forwarded to the correct destination.
configuring a switch with proper security to ensure that all traffic forwarded through an interface is filtered.
designing a network to use multiple virtual devices to ensure that all traffic uses the best path through the internetwork.
designing a network to use multiple paths between switches to ensure there is no single point of failure.
explain_short: Redundancy = multiple paths/components so a single failure does not break connectivity. Multiple physical paths between switches eliminate single points of failure.

stem: Refer to the exhibit. Which IP addressing scheme should be changed?
explain_short: Exhibit-based question. Photo unclear — choices not transcribed.

stem: Fill in the blank. When a router receives a packet, it examines the destination address of the packet and looks up this address in its _______ to determine the best path. (Likely 'routing table' or 'global configuration'.)
explain_short: Likely 'routing table' — that is what a router consults to determine the best path / next-hop for an incoming packet.
